creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
videos.summary: The Skill Checklist Framework provides a structured approach to building high-quality agent skills across four dimensions: Trigger (user vs. model-invoked), Structure (steps and references), Steering (leading words and cognitive load), and Pruning (removing legacy and no-op instructions). The framework addresses the practical problem of "skill hell" by establishing a shared rubric for developers building maintainable skills for AI agents.

videos.title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
videos.summary: RL Nabors argues that most applications default to frontier models unnecessarily, incurring latency, energy, and cost penalties when smaller local models would suffice. The talk covers evaluation vocabulary and frameworks for deciding when to deploy local versus frontier models, using production traces and LLM-as-judge techniques to measure model performance objectively.

videos.title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
videos.summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together represent the future of useful AI, enabling smaller models to handle real work and shifting AI from internal tools to customer-facing products. This architecture applies composition over inheritance to achieve better capability, reliability, and cost efficiency.

videos.title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
videos.summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI code generation shifts the bottleneck upstream from coding to requirements discovery and stakeholder alignment. Using a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned for non-technical reasons, he contends that human skills in reading requirements, mapping processes, and naming problems precisely remain irreplaceable. He recommends story mapping, value frameworks, and VAD thinking as practical tools to validate ideas before development.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model itself accounts for only 10% of results while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) determines the remaining 90%. He frames AI coding as a spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and identifies context engineering and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles as critical skills.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Top AI engineers use automated loops that prompt agents repeatedly rather than direct prompting, reducing manual overhead but increasing costs and hallucination risks. The approach involves orchestrators that spawn parallel worker agents to handle tasks, with observability dashboards for monitoring and control.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents and LLMs as interchangeable workers. It enables task routing across Claude Code, Codex, Pi, and other models with governance layers and cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 was pulled from availability three days after launch following a US government export-control directive over a claimed jailbreak vulnerability. The video discusses the regulatory action, reasons for immediate shutdown across all customers, and implications for developers. The author benchmarked Fable 5 before removal and compares its capabilities and pricing to Claude Opus 4.8.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: I was giving my coding agent context the wrong way...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a lesson on optimizing how context is provided to coding agents, suggesting prior practice was suboptimal. The content references a codebase harness skill and directs viewers to resources including the AI Builder Club and Superdesign tool.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses a new secret project from the OpenClaw creator involving Crabbox and a codebase harness available on GitHub. The content appears focused on tooling and setup for AI builders, with related references to Superdesign and the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason presents Loop Engineer as a tool for AI agent development. The video offers setup guidance and references a GitHub template, workshop materials, and related resources for builders using this framework.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive agents and self-improving companies using AI loops. It covers the conceptual foundations of proactive AI systems and demonstrates practical applications in autonomous ads optimization and SEO. The approach relies on memory, tools, and looping mechanisms to enable autonomous decision-making and continuous improvement.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: The video discusses OpenAI's announcement of GPT 5.6 and its regulatory status as the AI industry matures. It compares OpenAI's model to Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5, which were controversially paused, and notes OpenAI's consolidation of models into three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Educational overview of generative pretrained transformer fundamentals covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, and layer normalization. The video explains how transformers like GPT power leading models including Gemini, Opus, and Qwen through basic mechanics and mathematical operations.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Educational explanation of bigram language models covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization. Positions bigrams as foundational to understanding transformer architectures like GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer, mixture of experts (latent MoE), and multi-token prediction architectures to differentiate from other open models.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Containers on Amazon ECS with Mama J
videos.summary: AWS Developers video explaining containers, Docker, and Amazon ECS orchestration. Covers the problem containers solve, Docker image building, ECS management approaches (EC2 vs Fargate), and includes a real-world shopping app example with auto-scaling.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: A system design tutorial that teaches a 7-step framework for approaching design problems: requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack, and implementation constraints. The author argues that most tutorials wrongly start with technology rather than understanding the problem first.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: AWS Developers explains how to enable AI agents with tool-calling capabilities to move beyond stateless chatbots. Tools consist of a schema, description, and handler function that allow agents to request actions, which a framework executes in a loop, with system prompts guiding when each tool is invoked.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS gives AI coding agents access to up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It reduces reliance on agents' general knowledge and improves success rates when working with AWS services.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines why AI has become expensive, analyzing trends including tokenmaxxing, token time inflation, and automation time costs. The video draws on The Pragmatic Engineer's analysis of tokenmaxxing as a strategy in the AI industry and explores the underlying economic drivers of rising AI costs.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers use code generation effectively, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code and examining Google's own code generation approach across 2024-2026. The video frames code generation adoption through the lens of which tools engineers actually use in practice.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source, discussing its implications for open source maintainers and the ecosystem. The video frames the topic as a provocative debate about whether Malus represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: Career and Leadership Advice for Anyone Feeling Behind in the AI Era | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
videos.summary: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, discusses career and leadership lessons from his experience at Google, founding the failed search engine Neeva, and now leading Snowflake through the AI era. He shares principles on product building, hiring adaptable talent, staying close to the product, and maintaining identity beyond successes and failures.

videos.title: Everything You Should Know Before Mythos Arrives | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
videos.summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has dramatically reduced the cost and complexity of launching cyberattacks, requiring defenders to shift from reactive alerting to proactive threat hunting. He advocates for an "assume breach" posture and identifies three behavioral signals that reveal attacker presence in networks.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, co-founder of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that the "SaaSpocalypse" narrative about AI disrupting SaaS companies is overstated and that the best AI products build raving fans rather than just satisfied customers.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the humanoid robotics focus by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision. She contends the real challenge for widespread robot adoption is designing for human interaction and delight rather than utility alone, and that billions of robots will need to prioritize collaboration over autonomous capability.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Learns, Takes Initiative, Works Asynchronously: The Future is Here! #shorts
videos.summary: The video claims that AI systems are learning, taking initiative, and operating asynchronously in ambient mode with organizational learning capabilities. This is presented as a future state that is arriving now.

videos.title: Claude Tag: AI's European Takeover in Microsoft Teams! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses the integration of Claude into Microsoft Teams and Slack, questioning whether this represents a strategic marketing initiative aimed at enterprise adoption in Europe. The content frames the move as a potential sophisticated play for SaaS market penetration.

videos.title: AI's Model Context Protocols: The Future Revealed! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof explores Model Context Protocols (MCPs) and their role in AI development. The video examines current applications and speculates on future advancements in how AI systems manage and utilize contextual information.

videos.title: Unlock AI Power: Keep Clients Paying With External Agent Access #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that companies can stay competitive and retain clients by integrating external AI agents like Claude through Model Context Protocols, ensuring continued license payments. This positions external agent access as a client retention tactic.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
